evidence: The paper employs Ranger (Random Forest) alongside CatBoost, GBM, and logistic regression but reports only predictive performance: "All models had sensitivities, specificities and AUCs with ranges of 0.51-0.86, from 0.52 to 0.83, and 0.71-0.74, respectively." Risk factor conclusions derive from multivariable logistic regression: "In multivariable analysis, specialisation other than gastroenterology...previous inadequate bowel preparation (aOR 2.27, 95% CI 1.74-2.96), an aggregated comorbidity score >=1 (aOR 1.58...)." No variable importance rankings or interpretations from tree-based models appear in the paper.
